text.title: Sidekiq Jobs Anti-Patterns
text.summary: ActiveRecord args, swallowed exceptions, ActiveJob API confusion, missing retry/dead, WithoutOverlapping cargo-cult.

antipattern.description: Passing ActiveRecord model instances as perform arguments: Sidekiq 7 strict mode raises on enqueue; legacy versions serialize via GlobalID but the object may be stale on dequeue.
antipattern.reason: JSON-only serialization is Sidekiq's contact; GlobalID round-trips re-fetch on dequeue but fail on soft-deleted or destroyed records. Always pass the ID.

antipattern.description: Rescuing StandardError inside perform and not re-raising: the job always appears to succeed, retry never fires, and failures are invisible in the dead set.
antipattern.reason: Sidekiq retries a job when perform raises. Swallowing the exception removes the signal. Only rescue exceptions you intend to recover from; always re-raise the rest.

antipattern.description: Using perform_now or queue_as from the ActiveJob API instead of perform_async or sidekiq_options from the native Sidekiq API.
antipattern.reason: ActiveJob wraps Sidekiq; its adapter serializes differently, adds adapter overhead, and has different retry semantics. For non-trivial Sidekiq use, the native API gives full control of queue weighting, retry curves, and dead-set behavior.

antipattern.description: Omitting sidekiq_options retry: N and dead: true, relying on the default 25 retries over 21 days.
antipattern.reason: 21 days of retries is meaningless for an order confirmation email; it fills the retry set and makes monitoring noisy. Always declare intent explicitly.

antipattern.description: Using WithoutOverlapping as if it were a Sidekiq built-in; it is a Laravel term — Sidekiq requires sidekiq-unique-jobs or a hand-rolled Redis lock for deduplication.
antipattern.reason: LLMs cargo-cult Laravel queue middleware syntax into Sidekiq code; WithoutOverlapping does not exist in Sidekiq core and will raise a NoMethodError.

antipattern.description: Assuming perform_in with a past timestamp defers the job to "never" — it runs immediately.
antipattern.reason: Sidekiq executes jobs scheduled in the past immediately on the next worker poll. Only future timestamps create a deferral; past timestamps are treated as "run ASAP."
